intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Twenty years as a commercial pilot, Vern. Last week I saw something that shouldn't exist.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Twenty years. That's a lot of flight hours.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Forty thousand hours. I know every aircraft type. This was none of them.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Describe it.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Tic-tac shape. No wings, no exhaust. It matched our speed at 35,000 feet, then accelerated vertically and was gone in two seconds.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Military has classified aircraft. Could be something experimental.

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: Nothing we have can accelerate like that. The G-forces would kill a pilot.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: Forty thousand hours. You're not easily fooled.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: I reported it to the airline. They took it seriously. First time that's ever happened.

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Thank you for coming forward. This is important.

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: Someone needs to talk about this. Thanks, Vern.

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Retired Navy pilot here. Saw something on a training flight that changed my mind about all this.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Navy pilot. Where was this?

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Off the coast, 2015. Object descended from 80,000 feet to sea level in less than a second.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: That's impossible.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: I know. I was on the radar. So were five other pilots. We all saw it.

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: Multiple independent systems, visual confirmation, and it's on film.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: Multiple witnesses, radar, and film. That's as solid as it gets.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: The Pentagon acknowledged it last year. Finally.

intro.speaker: Vern
intro.text: {callerName} from {callerLocation}, I understand you have professional experience in aviation?

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Air traffic controller for fifteen years, Vern. I've tracked something I can't explain.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Tell me about it.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Object appeared on radar at 40,000 feet. No transponder, no flight plan. It descended to 100 feet in four seconds.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: That rate of descent would destroy any conventional aircraft.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Correct. Then it hovered over the water for six minutes and shot back up. Multiple controllers witnessed it.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Any chance of radar spoofing or electronic warfare testing?

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: We considered that. Coast Guard sent a boat. They saw it visually before it disappeared.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: Visual confirmation from Coast Guard. That's significant.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: The official report was classified. But we know what we saw.

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Thank you for your testimony. This is exactly the kind of credible account we need.

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: Someone should be asking questions. Appreciate you doing that, Vern.

intro.speaker: Vern
intro.text: {callerName} from {callerLocation}, you've got credentials. Let's hear it.

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Aerospace engineer, twenty-two years. What I saw last month defied everything I know about physics.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: An engineer saying something defied physics. That's significant.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Metallic sphere, maybe 30 feet diameter. It was rotating on multiple axes simultaneously while stationary.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: That shouldn't be possible.

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: It's not. Then it accelerated from zero to hypersonic with no sonic boom. I had my telescope on it.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Could it have been some kind of optical illusion? Atmospheric effects?

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: I've considered every explanation. None of them fit the data.

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: An aerospace engineer with a telescope. This is credible testimony.

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: I didn't want to believe it. But the evidence is undeniable.

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: Thank you for coming forward. Your expertise matters.

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: The scientific community needs to take this seriously. Thanks for the platform, Vern.

intro.speaker: Vern
intro.text: {callerName} from {callerLocation}! I hear you've got serious credentials!

intro.speaker: Caller
intro.text: Former Air Force, Vern. What I witnessed is still classified, but I'm talking anyway.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: Tell us what you can!

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: Object tracked on radar doing impossible maneuvers. 90-degree turns at Mach 5. Multiple F-18s scrambled.

development.speaker: Vern
development.text: F-18s! What happened when they intercepted?!

development.speaker: Caller
development.text: It played with them. Accelerated away, then reappeared behind them. The pilots were shaken.

Skeptical.speaker: Vern
Skeptical.text: Could this have been a secret military test?!

Skeptical.speaker: Caller
Skeptical.text: We WERE the military! This wasn't ours! The brass was panicked!

Believing.speaker: Vern
Believing.text: Outmaneuvering F-18s! This is exactly what other pilots have reported!

Believing.speaker: Caller
Believing.text: It's happening more often! Someone is watching us!

conclusion.speaker: Vern
conclusion.text: This is incredible testimony! Thank you for your courage!

conclusion.speaker: Caller
conclusion.text: The truth needs to come out! Thanks, Vern!
